description_en: A lively, Showa-era izakaya in the heart of Omoide Yokocho (Memory Lane), serving yakitori skewers for over 70 years. The open-air seating and nostalgic alley atmosphere make it a must-visit for first-time Tokyo visitors.
description_ja: 新宿・思い出横丁の中心にある、70年以上続く昭和レトロな居酒屋。名物のしゃもじつくねをはじめ、リーズナブルな焼き鳥が楽しめます。
booking_tips_en: No reservation needed. Just walk in and grab a seat at the counter. Peak hours (7-9pm) can be crowded, so arrive early.

description_en: A popular seafood izakaya steps from Shinjuku Station, famous for its generous sashimi platters starting at just 1,000 yen. The lively atmosphere and fresh daily catches make it a local favorite for after-work drinks.
description_ja: 新宿駅からすぐの人気海鮮居酒屋。1,000円からの豪快な刺身盛りが名物で、毎日届く新鮮な魚介が自慢。仕事帰りの一杯に地元客で賑わいます。
booking_tips_en: Online reservation recommended via Tabelog or Hot Pepper. Walk-ins possible but expect a wait on weekday evenings.

description_en: A robatayaki paradise for sake lovers in Nishi-Shinjuku, with over 50 premium sake varieties selected by certified sommeliers. Watch chefs grill seasonal seafood over charcoal while sipping warm sake from traditional copper vessels.
description_ja: 西新宿の炉端焼きと日本酒の名店。利き酒師が厳選した50種以上の銘酒と、炭火で焼き上げる旬の海鮮を堪能できます。伝統的な銅製の燗徳利で味わう温かい日本酒は格別。
booking_tips_en: Reservation recommended, especially on weekends. Book via their official website or Tabelog. Counter seats offer the best robatayaki viewing.
address_en: B1F Shinjuku Vintage Bldg, 7-8-6 Nishi-Shinjuku, Shinjuku-ku, Tokyo
address_ja: 東京都新宿区西新宿7-8-6 新宿ヴィンテージビルB1F

name_ja: 半兵ヱ 新宿歌舞伎町店
description_en: A retro-themed izakaya in Kabukicho that transports you to 1950s Japan, with yakitori from just 60 yen per skewer and drinks from 300 yen. The Showa-era decor and incredibly cheap prices make it perfect for budget travelers.
description_ja: 歌舞伎町にある昭和レトロな居酒屋。焼き鳥1本60円〜、ドリンク300円〜と驚きの安さ。レトロな店内で昭和の雰囲気を楽しみながらお酒が飲めます。
booking_tips_en: No reservation needed. Open late on weekends (until 5am on Saturdays). Great for late-night snacking in Kabukicho.
address_en: 5F Daiichi Metro Bldg, 1-23-14 Kabukicho, Shinjuku-ku, Tokyo
address_ja: 東京都新宿区歌舞伎町1-23-14 第一メトロビル5F

description_en: A hidden upscale izakaya in a basement near Shibuya Station, featuring an open kitchen with creative Japanese cuisine. The inventive menu includes dishes like vegetable crudite with tomato miso and yuzu-miso marinated grilled chicken.
description_ja: 渋谷駅近くの地下に佇む隠れ家的な和食居酒屋。オープンキッチンで仕上げる創作和食が自慢。トマト味噌のバーニャカウダや柚子味噌チキンなど独創的なメニューが楽しめます。
booking_tips_en: Reservation strongly recommended via phone (03-3797-1911) or online platforms like JPNEAZY. The basement entrance is easy to miss — look for the stairs.
address_ja: 東京都渋谷区渋谷3-5-5 B1F

name_ja: 渋谷のんべい横丁 立ち飲み屋
description_en: A tiny standing-bar izakaya tucked inside Nonbei Yokocho (Drunkard's Alley), one of Shibuya's last remaining retro drinking alleys. With only a handful of seats, you'll be rubbing elbows with locals over cheap drinks and simple grilled snacks.
description_ja: 渋谷・のんべい横丁にある小さな立ち飲み居酒屋。数席しかない店内で地元の常連客と肩を寄せ合いながら、安くて旨い酒と焼き物を楽しめます。
booking_tips_en: Walk-in only. The alley is right next to Shibuya Station's east exit, under the train tracks. Most shops close by 11pm.
address_ja: 東京都渋谷区渋谷1-25 のんべい横丁

description_en: An exclusive, reservations-only izakaya hidden on a Shibuya back street, known among locals for its exquisite seasonal omakase and rare sake pairings. The intimate counter seats only 8 guests, and the chef personally crafts each course.
description_ja: 渋谷の裏路地に佇む完全予約制の隠れ家居酒屋。季節のおまかせコースと希少な日本酒のペアリングが評判。カウンター8席のみで、大将が一品一品仕立てます。
booking_tips_en: Reservation required by phone only (Japanese). Extremely hard to book — regulars get priority. Try calling right when phone lines open. No walk-ins accepted.

description_en: An intimate fish-and-sake izakaya on a quiet Ebisu side street, renowned for its eclectic list of 42 sakes and fresh fish dishes like fuwa-fuwa satsuma-age and namero. Counter seats let you watch the chef and chat about sake.
description_ja: 恵比寿の路地裏にある魚と日本酒の名店。42種の個性的な日本酒と、ふわふわさつま揚げや鳴門など新鮮な魚料理が自慢。カウンター席で板前との会話も楽しめます。
booking_tips_en: Reservations recommended, especially for counter seats. Book via Tabelog. Entrance is up a steep staircase on the 2nd floor — easy to miss.

description_en: A Michelin-recognized izakaya hidden behind an unmarked concrete wall near the Shibuya River. Chef-driven seasonal kaiseki-style plates are served at a wooden counter overlooking the open kitchen. Reservation-only; no sign outside.
description_ja: 渋谷川沿いの無印コンクリート壁の奥に佇むミシュラン掲載居酒屋。オープンキッチンを望む木のカウンターで、季節の懐石風小皿を堪能。完全予約制、看板なし。
booking_tips_en: Reservation required — phone only (Japanese). Book at least 2-3 weeks ahead. The entrance is a featureless sliding metal door past Ebisu Bridge; no sign marks it.
address_ja: 東京都渋谷区広尾1-12-15 リバーサイドビル1F

name_ja: わらやき屋 六本木
description_en: A dramatic izakaya specializing in straw-fire grilled Kochi cuisine, where chefs sear bonito at 900 degrees Celsius in front of you. The signature katsuo no shio tataki (straw-seared bonito with salt) is a must-try.
description_ja: 900度の藁焼きで豪快に仕上げる高知料理の専門店。目の前で焼き上げる名物「鰹の塩たたき」は必食。土佐の食材と地酒を六本木で堪能できます。
booking_tips_en: Book online via Gurunavi or their website. Counter seats near the straw grill are most popular. English menu available.

description_en: The iconic two-story izakaya famously featured in Quentin Tarantino's Kill Bill and site of the Bush-Koizumi 'Izakaya Summit.' Soba noodles, robatayaki, and sushi are served in a stunning traditional interior with soaring ceilings.
description_ja: 映画『キル・ビル』のモデルとなった二階建ての名物居酒屋。ブッシュ・小泉「居酒屋サミット」の舞台としても有名。蕎麦、炉端焼き、寿司を圧巻の伝統的空間で楽しめます。
booking_tips_en: Book online via their website. Open until 3am, making it great for late-night dining. English menu and English-speaking staff available.

description_en: A traditional izakaya in a 65-year-old townhouse serving Edo-period cuisine, most famous for its negima nabe (tuna and green onion hot pot) made with heirloom Edo vegetables. The classical facade and tatami rooms create an authentic old-Tokyo atmosphere.
description_ja: 築65年の町家で営む伝統的な居酒屋。江戸野菜を使った名物「ねぎま鍋」をはじめ、江戸の庶民料理を現代に伝える。畳の個室や古き良き東京の雰囲気が魅力。
booking_tips_en: Phone reservation recommended for course meals and groups. Japanese language required for booking. Walk-ins may be possible for a la carte on weekdays.

description_en: A lively, old-school izakaya on Hoppy Street near Sensoji Temple, where locals and tourists gather at outdoor tables for cheap hoppy beer and grilled skewers. The festive street atmosphere is quintessential Asakusa.
description_ja: 浅草ホッピー通りにある活気あふれる昔ながらの居酒屋。名物のホッピーと焼き鳥を屋外テーブルで楽しめます。浅草らしいお祭りのような雰囲気が魅力。
booking_tips_en: No reservation needed. Just grab an outdoor seat on Hoppy Street. Open from early afternoon — perfect for day drinking near Sensoji Temple.
address_ja: 東京都台東区浅草2-3-10（ホッピー通り）

description_en: A hidden basement izakaya off Rikkyo Street specializing in Daisen chicken from Tottori, hand-skewered and grilled over premium Kishu binchotan charcoal. The private rooms make it ideal for intimate dinners.
description_ja: 立教通り脇の地下に佇む隠れ家居酒屋。鳥取県の大山鶏を一本一本丁寧に串打ちし、紀州備長炭で焼き上げる本格焼き鳥。個室完備で接待にも最適。
booking_tips_en: Phone reservation recommended, especially for private rooms. Japanese language needed for booking. The basement entrance is easy to miss.
address_en: B1F Daichiya Bldg, 3-29-12 Nishi-Ikebukuro, Toshima-ku, Tokyo
address_ja: 東京都豊島区西池袋3-29-12 大ちやビルB1F

description_en: A charming izakaya inside a renovated kominka (traditional Japanese house) that feels like a secret getaway from busy Ikebukuro. The Italian-influenced Japanese menu and calm atmosphere attract a regular local crowd.
description_ja: 古民家をリノベーションした池袋の隠れ家的居酒屋。イタリアンの要素を取り入れた和食メニューと落ち着いた雰囲気が常連客に愛されています。
booking_tips_en: Book via Tabelog or Gurunavi. Lunch service available on weekdays. A 3-minute walk from Ikebukuro Station south exit.
address_ja: 東京都豊島区南池袋1-17-2 INビル3F

description_en: A legendary open-air izakaya on Ameyoko street serving grilled offal and stewed horse innards since 1950. Tightly packed counter seats face the bustling market street. Come for the atmosphere and absurdly cheap drinks.
description_ja: 1950年創業、アメ横の名物居酒屋。もつ焼きと馬もつ煮込みが看板メニュー。市場通りに面したカウンターで、活気ある雰囲気と格安の酒を楽しめます。
booking_tips_en: Walk-in only. Opens at 10am — perfect for daytime drinking. Very crowded on weekends. No English menu but pointing at dishes works fine.

description_en: A bustling standing-bar izakaya in Ueno where 1,000 yen gets you a drink and two dishes. Popular with local office workers and tourists alike, it offers a no-fuss, authentic tachinomi (standing bar) experience near Ameyoko.
description_ja: 1,000円でドリンク1杯とおつまみ2品が楽しめる上野の人気立ち飲み居酒屋。サラリーマンや観光客で賑わう本格的な立ち飲み体験ができます。
booking_tips_en: No reservation needed or accepted. Standing room fills up fast after 5pm on weekdays. Table seats available at the back if you prefer sitting.

description_en: A beloved Shimokitazawa izakaya tucked in the backstreets, famous for its sunken central kitchen with simmering oden pots and creative twists on classics like nikujaga with garlic bread and tofu cheese with honey. The sake list is excellent and ever-changing.
description_ja: 下北沢の路地裏にある人気居酒屋。中央の厨房でコトコト煮込むおでんが名物。ニクジャガをガーリックブレッドと合わせるなどのアレンジ料理と、厳選された日本酒が楽しめます。
booking_tips_en: Phone reservation recommended (Japanese only). Very popular on weekends — try weekday evenings for easier access. Counter seats near the oden pot are the best.

description_en: A chef-driven izakaya where Satoshi-san crafts an omakase menu of exquisite small plates paired with carefully selected sake. The intimate third-floor setting and personalized service make every visit unique. Reservations are essential.
description_ja: 大将のさとしさんが仕立てるおまかせ小皿料理と厳選日本酒のペアリングが絶品。3階の隠れ家的空間で、一人一人に合わせたおもてなしが受けられる特別な居酒屋。
booking_tips_en: Phone reservation required (Japanese preferred). Omakase is the way to go — let chef Satoshi choose for you. Book at least 1 week ahead.

description_en: A refined standing izakaya in a Ginza back street, run by former Michelin-starred kaiseki chef Hiroshi Shimada. The legendary karasumi soba (cold noodles with grated bottarga) and daily-changing seasonal menu draw a devoted following. Only 14 seats.
description_ja: ミシュラン星付き懐石出身の島田裕司氏が営む銀座裏通りの立ち飲み居酒屋。名物「からすみ蕎麦」と日替わりの季節メニューが通を唸らせる。わずか14席。
booking_tips_en: Reservation required for peak hours. Phone only (Japanese). The standing-only table for 4 can be reserved; counter is first-come. Arrive right at opening for best chance.

description_en: One of Tokyo's oldest izakayas, founded in 1905, hidden on a quiet Kanda side street. Now in its fourth generation, the worn wooden interior and tatami rooms exude century-old character. Specialties include horse meat sashimi and loach hot pot (Yanagawa nabe).
description_ja: 1905年創業、神田の路地裏に佇む東京最古級の居酒屋。4代目が守る使い込まれた木造の店内と畳の座敷が100年の風格を漂わせる。馬刺しとどじょう鍋（柳川鍋）が名物。
booking_tips_en: Phone reservation strongly recommended (Japanese only). Lines form before 5pm opening. Closed Saturday lunch and all day Sunday. A true piece of Tokyo history.
address_ja: 東京都千代田区神田司町2-15-2

name_ja: あべちゃん 麻布十番店
description_en: A legendary yakitori izakaya founded in 1933, using the same secret sauce pot for over 90 years. The sweet-spicy tare on perfectly grilled chicken skewers is iconic in this upscale Azabu-Juban neighborhood. Also serves excellent yakiton (pork skewers).
description_ja: 1933年創業、90年以上同じタレ壺を使い続ける麻布十番の老舗焼き鳥居酒屋。甘辛いタレで焼き上げる絶品焼き鳥に加え、やきとん（豚串）も人気。下町の風情が残る名店。
booking_tips_en: Phone reservation recommended (Japanese). Opens at 3pm on weekdays — arrive early to avoid the after-work rush. Closed Sundays. Cash preferred.
